title: Kit Digital 2026: The Complete Guide for Spanish Businesses (€3,000–€29,000 in EU Grants)
description: A plain-English guide to Spain's Kit Digital 2026 for freelancers and SMEs: what it is, how much you get per segment (€3,000–€29,000), the requirements, and how to apply for the digital voucher.
dek: The EU is subsidising the digitalisation of Spanish businesses through the Kit Digital voucher. Here is the plain-English version: how much you get, who qualifies, and how to be ready so you don't miss out.
author: The Kodable team
takeaways: Kit Digital is an EU grant (NextGenerationEU): it gives you a voucher to digitalise your business, and you never pay it back.
The amount ranges from €3,000 (freelancers) to €29,000 (companies up to 250 employees), depending on your segment.
Applying is four steps: register on Acelera Pyme, do the self-assessment test, submit the application, and choose a digitiser.
In 2026 the programme runs until the funds run out, get ready now, because it is handed out first-come, first-served.

body.alt: Official emblem: Funded by the European Union, NextGenerationEU
body.caption: Kit Digital is financed by the European Union's NextGenerationEU funds.

body.text: The European Union is putting money on the table so that Spanish businesses go digital. It is called **Kit Digital**, and since 2022 it has handed out billions in vouchers for websites, online shops, cybersecurity and more. Most of the freelancers and small businesses that could claim it... simply don't. This guide explains, without the jargon, what it is, how much you get, and how to be ready so you are not left out.
What Kit Digital actually is, in plain terms

Kit Digital is a public grant programme from the Government of Spain, managed by **Red.es** and financed by the European [NextGenerationEU](https://espanadigital.gob.es/lineas-de-actuacion/programa-kit-digital) funds under the Recovery Plan. Translated: the administration gives you a **digital voucher** (a fixed amount of money) so you can hire digital solutions (a website, an online shop, a CRM, cybersecurity) from approved companies called **agentes digitalizadores** (digitisers). You never touch the cash: the voucher pays the solution's invoice directly.
It is not a loan. You do not pay it back. It is a subsidy, conditional on you genuinely digitalising your business.
How much you get: amounts by segment
What you can receive depends on the size of your workforce. The programme splits beneficiaries into **segments**:

body.alt: Chart of Kit Digital voucher amounts by company segment, from €3,000 to €29,000
body.caption: Maximum voucher by headcount, from Segment III (freelancers) to Segment V (up to 250 employees).

items: **Segment III** (0–2 employees, most freelancers): up to **€3,000**.

body.label: the maximum voucher, for medium-sized companies of up to 250 employees (Segment V)

body.text: The important shift of the last couple of years? The programme **raised the Segment III voucher to €3,000** and **widened its reach to companies of up to 250 employees** (Segments IV and V), which used to be reserved for the smallest businesses. More and more companies now qualify.
Can you claim it? The basic requirements
Kit Digital is aimed at freelancers, micro-businesses and SMEs with a tax address in Spain. Broadly, you need to:

items: Be a **registered autónomo** or an SME/micro-business with fewer than 250 employees.
Have a **minimum trading history** (usually 6 months registered in the RETA or as a company).
Be **up to date** with your tax (Hacienda) and Social Security obligations.
Not exceed the accumulated **de minimis aid** limits of recent years.

body.title: An honest note
body.body: The exact requirements (trading history, de minimis amounts, deadlines) are set by each call (convocatoria) in its own rules. Before you take anything for granted, confirm it in the [official rules](https://www.boe.es/buscar/doc.php?id=BOE-A-2026-2070) and on [acelerapyme.gob.es](https://www.acelerapyme.gob.es/kit-digital). We give you the map; the fine print always lives at the official source.

body.text: How to apply, step by step

body.alt: The four steps to apply for Kit Digital: register, self-assessment, application, digitiser
body.caption: The process end to end: registration, self-assessment, application, and choosing your digitiser.

steps.title: Register on Acelera Pyme
steps.body: Go to acelerapyme.gob.es and create your account with a digital certificate or Cl@ve. It is the front door to the whole process.

steps.title: Take the self-assessment test
steps.body: A short questionnaire (around 13 questions) that measures your digital maturity. There is no pass mark (you just have to do it) but it is mandatory in order to claim the voucher.

steps.title: Apply for your digital voucher
steps.body: When your call is open, you submit the application on the Red.es electronic office, in the procedure for your segment.

steps.title: Choose your digitiser
steps.body: Once the voucher is granted, you pick a company from the official catalogue of agentes digitalizadores, who delivers the solution and invoices against your voucher.

body.text: What can you spend the voucher on?
The voucher is not free cash: it is spent on **categories of solutions** from the official catalogue. The most useful ones for a local business:

body.alt: Categories of digital solutions funded by Kit Digital, including website, e-commerce, CRM, cybersecurity and AI
body.caption: Some of the eligible solutions from the official catalogue.

items: **Website and online presence**: a professional site so people find you on Google.
**E-commerce**: selling online, with a catalogue and a payment gateway.
**Social media** and **marketing** management to win customers.
**Customer management (CRM)** so no opportunity slips through.
**Cybersecurity** and **secure workplace** to protect your data.
**E-invoicing**, **virtual office** and **process management** to save hours every week.

body.text: And the direction of travel is clear: the programme keeps adding **artificial intelligence** and managed-cybersecurity solutions, exactly where a small business gains the most time.
The 2026 status: is it open right now?

Here we have to be straight, because there is a lot of confusing information out there. The original Kit Digital calls (Segments I, II and III) **closed in October 2025**. In January 2026, the official gazette published [Orden TDF/39/2026](https://www.boe.es/buscar/doc.php?id=BOE-A-2026-2070), which **reactivates the programme's legal framework** and allows the **leftover European funds** from earlier calls to be redistributed.

What does that mean for you today? That the programme **drops the rigid closing date** and instead runs **until the funds are exhausted**. New calls are published as those remaining funds get reassigned. Which is why the real status changes from week to week: the only reliable answer is the official source.

body.title: Where to check the real status
body.body: Always look at the Red.es electronic office, [sede.red.gob.es](https://sede.red.gob.es), and [acelerapyme.gob.es](https://www.acelerapyme.gob.es/kit-digital) before acting. If your call is open, the deadline is shown there; if not, it appears as closed or coming soon.

body.text: Why get ready TODAY, even if your call is not open

There is a lesson from the previous calls worth burning into memory: **the money is handed out in order of arrival, until it runs out.** The businesses that already had their registration done, their self-assessment passed and their digitiser chosen were able to apply the moment the window opened. The ones who started finding out that day arrived too late.

items: Create your account and **do the self-assessment now**: it is free and does not expire.
Decide **which solutions** you want to fund (website, online shop, CRM, cybersecurity).
Have **your digitiser identified** so you are not improvising on opening day.

body.text: The voucher does not go to whoever needs it most, but to whoever shows up ready on the day it opens.
How Kodable helps

We build exactly the kind of solutions Kit Digital subsidises: **websites that sell, online shops, AI agents, automations and custom tools** for local businesses. We help you reach opening day with everything ready, the registration, the self-assessment, and a clear plan for what to build with your voucher.

An honest note on the fine print: for the voucher to pay the invoice, the solution must be delivered by an **agente digitalizador from the official catalogue**. We walk the whole path with you (design, build and launch) and guide you to the right digitiser when the moment comes. No promises we can't keep.

body.title: Get ready for your Kit Digital
body.body: Tell us about your business in a 15-minute call. We'll tell you what you can fund and leave your application ready for when your call opens.

body.text: Frequently asked questions
Do you have to pay Kit Digital back?
No. It is a subsidy, not a loan. If you meet the conditions and justify the solution, there is nothing to repay.
How much can a freelancer with no employees claim?
A freelancer in Segment III (0–2 employees) can receive up to **€3,000** in digital voucher.
Can I choose any company to build my website?
Only if it is registered as an **agente digitalizador** in the official Red.es catalogue. The funded solution must be delivered by an approved digitiser.
Is the application window open right now?

It depends on the call and the moment. The framework has been reactivated since 2026 and runs until funds are exhausted; check the status on [sede.red.gob.es](https://sede.red.gob.es) and [acelerapyme.gob.es](https://www.acelerapyme.gob.es/kit-digital).
